```python
# Signed: MurphySig Example + claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, 2024-10-22
# Format: MurphySig v0.3.3 (https://murphysig.dev/spec)
# Prior: Unknown (no signature existed before this edit)
#
# Context: A simple slugify function for converting strings to URL-safe slugs
#
# Confidence: 0.9 - Function is straightforward but lacks comprehensive Unicode handling
# Open: Should this handle Unicode characters? What about multiple languages?

def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert a string to a URL-safe slug."""
    import re
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    text = text.strip("-")
    return text
```
